slides.title: Connection may rival *diet and exercise* for a long life.
slides.sub: Strong relationships are one of the clearest predictors of healthy aging. The good news: small habits build them.

slides.title: Ties add years
slides.body: Strong social ties are *linked to longer life*. In long studies they hold up alongside the usual health habits.

slides.title: Loneliness has weight
slides.body: Loneliness carries a risk that researchers *compare to smoking*. It is a health factor, not just a mood.

slides.title: Quality over crowd
slides.body: It is *quality and regularity* that matter, not the size of the crowd. A few steady relationships go a long way.

slides.title: Build small rituals
slides.body: Make connection a habit: a *weekly call or shared meal*. Routine keeps relationships alive without much planning.

slides.title: It softens stress
slides.body: Connection also *buffers everyday stress*. People you can lean on take the edge off ordinary hard days.

slides.title: Tend the habits that *add good years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the sleep, food, movement and steps that support healthy aging, all in one calm daily number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Strong connection may rival diet and exercise for a long life, and loneliness carries a risk researchers compare to smoking. 🤝

It is quality and regularity that matter, not crowd size. Build small rituals: a weekly call, a shared meal. Connection also buffers everyday stress. Save this. 🔖
